_comment: State Medicare Advantage generation queue. Each entry produces ONE JSON file under content/data/medicare-advantage/<slug>.json via the coveredusa-ma-state-writer agent. Status starts as 'queued', flips to 'writing' when picked up, 'verified' when verifier approves, 'flagged' when verifier flags. Target: 50 states + DC = 51 total. California shipped as gold standard.

states.name: California
states.notes: Gold standard. Kaiser dominant. 402 plans, 3.6M enrollees, 51% penetration, $11 avg premium. Verified against KFF 2026 Spotlight 2026-05-14.

states.name: Texas
states.notes: Second-largest MA market by enrollee count. No Kaiser. Strong Humana + UnitedHealthcare presence. Big rural/urban variance (Houston vs West TX).

states.name: Florida
states.notes: Highest MA penetration nationally (~58%). Big retiree market. Humana, UnitedHealthcare, Florida Blue dominant. Hurricane SEP provisions worth mentioning.

states.name: New York
states.notes: Large NYC market + upstate rural. EmblemHealth, Wellcare, Humana, UnitedHealthcare. Higher premiums than national average. State-based ACA exchange (relevant for relatedLinks).

states.name: Pennsylvania
states.notes: Highmark Blue Cross dominant. Strong Humana presence. Industrial population, aging demographics. State-based ACA exchange (Pennie).

states.name: Wyoming
states.notes: Stress test: smallest MA market. Low penetration (~25%). Limited carrier choice. Many counties may have 5 or fewer plans. Non-expansion Medicaid state. Skip countyVariance section if data sparse.

states.name: Ohio
states.notes: Large industrial state. Anthem Blue Cross, Humana, Aetna strong. Healthy MA penetration.

states.name: Michigan
states.notes: Aging industrial population, high MA penetration. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Michigan dominant. Detroit urban + UP rural variance.

states.name: Arizona
states.notes: Big retiree destination. UnitedHealthcare, Humana, Banner|Aetna. Phoenix urban + tribal-area rural counties.

states.name: Georgia
states.notes: Kaiser operates here (Atlanta metro). Plus Humana, UnitedHealthcare, Anthem. Non-expansion Medicaid (relevant for D-SNP framing).

states.name: North Carolina
states.notes: Growing market. UnitedHealthcare, Humana, Aetna, Blue Cross NC. Recent Medicaid expansion (Dec 2023) changes D-SNP landscape.

states.name: Illinois
states.notes: Chicago urban + downstate rural. Aetna, Humana, Health Alliance, Blue Cross. State-based ACA exchange (Get Covered Illinois).

states.name: New Jersey
states.notes: Horizon Blue Cross dominant. High premiums vs national avg. State-based ACA exchange (Get Covered NJ). High population density.

states.name: Virginia
states.notes: Kaiser operates here (DC metro). Plus Humana, UnitedHealthcare, Anthem. Recent Medicaid expansion.

states.name: Washington
states.notes: Kaiser dominant (formerly Group Health). Premera Blue Cross strong. State-based ACA exchange. High Star Ratings statewide.

states.name: Massachusetts
states.notes: Health Connector state. Blue Cross of Massachusetts strong. Higher premiums than national. Universal coverage culture predates ACA.

states.name: Tennessee
states.notes: BlueCross BlueShield of Tennessee dominant. Humana, UnitedHealthcare. Non-expansion Medicaid.

states.name: Indiana
states.notes: Anthem Blue Cross strong (HQ here). HMO and PPO mix. Healthy industrial demographics.

states.name: Missouri
states.notes: Anthem, Humana, Aetna. Kansas City + St Louis + rural Ozarks variance. Recent Medicaid expansion (2021).

states.name: Maryland
states.notes: Kaiser operates here (DC metro). Maryland-specific all-payer hospital rates affect cost picture. State-based ACA exchange.

states.name: Wisconsin
states.notes: Anthem Blue Cross, UnitedHealthcare, Humana, plus local plans (Quartz, Network Health). Non-expansion Medicaid. Higher Star Ratings statewide.

states.name: Colorado
states.notes: Kaiser operates here (Front Range). Strong UnitedHealthcare. Mountain rural counties have fewer plans. State-based ACA exchange (Connect for Health Colorado).

states.name: Minnesota
states.notes: Unique state: nonprofit-only carriers (BlueCross MN, HealthPartners, Medica, UCare). Higher Star Ratings. State-based ACA exchange (MNsure).

states.name: South Carolina
states.notes: BlueCross BlueShield SC strong. Humana, UnitedHealthcare, Aetna. Non-expansion Medicaid.

states.name: Alabama
states.notes: BlueCross BlueShield Alabama very dominant. UnitedHealthcare, Humana. Non-expansion Medicaid.

states.name: Louisiana
states.notes: Blue Cross Blue Shield Louisiana, Humana, UnitedHealthcare. Recent Medicaid expansion (2016). Higher D-SNP relevance.

states.name: Kentucky
states.notes: Humana HQ is here, strong presence. Anthem Blue Cross, UnitedHealthcare. State-based ACA exchange (kynect).

states.name: Oregon
states.notes: Kaiser operates here (NW region). PacificSource, Providence, Moda. State-based ACA exchange.

states.name: Oklahoma
states.notes: BlueCross BlueShield Oklahoma, Humana, Aetna. Recent Medicaid expansion (2021). Tribal health coordination unique.

states.name: Connecticut
states.notes: ConnectiCare, Aetna (HQ here), Anthem. State-based ACA exchange (Access Health CT). High premiums vs national.

states.name: Utah
states.notes: SelectHealth (Intermountain) dominant. Younger demographic, lower MA penetration. Mountain rural variance.

states.name: Iowa
states.notes: Wellmark Blue Cross strong. Aging population. Rural-heavy state.

states.name: Nevada
states.notes: UnitedHealthcare, Humana, Anthem, Senior Care Plus. State-based ACA exchange (Nevada Health Link). Las Vegas urban concentrated.

states.name: Arkansas
states.notes: BlueCross BlueShield Arkansas, Humana, UnitedHealthcare. Smaller market.

states.name: Mississippi
states.notes: BlueCross BlueShield Mississippi, Humana, UnitedHealthcare. Non-expansion Medicaid. Low MA penetration.

states.name: Kansas
states.notes: BlueCross BlueShield Kansas, Humana, Aetna. Non-expansion Medicaid. Rural-heavy.

states.name: New Mexico
states.notes: Presbyterian, Blue Cross, UnitedHealthcare. State-based ACA exchange (beWellnm). High tribal population.

states.name: Nebraska
states.notes: Blue Cross Nebraska, Humana, Aetna. Rural-heavy. Smaller market.

states.name: West Virginia
states.notes: Highmark Blue Cross, Humana. Aging population, opioid-impacted. Higher D-SNP demand.

states.name: Idaho
states.notes: Blue Cross Idaho, Regence, Humana. State-based ACA exchange (Your Health Idaho). Mountain rural variance.

states.name: Hawaii
states.notes: Kaiser dominant. HMSA Blue Cross. Unique geography (interisland coverage). Very high MA penetration. Skip rural countyVariance, different county geography. Spanish: Hawái.

states.name: New Hampshire
states.notes: Anthem Blue Cross, Humana, Wellpoint. Small population, limited plan choice. New Hampshire ACA uses federal platform.

states.name: Maine
states.notes: Anthem Blue Cross, Humana, Martin's Point. State-based ACA exchange (CoverME.gov). Rural-heavy.

states.name: Montana
states.notes: Blue Cross Blue Shield Montana, Humana. Rural-heavy. Low MA penetration. Skip countyVariance if data sparse.

states.name: Delaware
states.notes: Highmark Blue Cross, Aetna, Humana. Very small market. Skip countyVariance (only 3 counties).

states.name: Rhode Island
states.notes: BlueCross BlueShield RI, UnitedHealthcare. State-based ACA exchange (HealthSource RI). Small population, urban-concentrated.

states.name: South Dakota
states.notes: Avera, Sanford, Wellmark Blue Cross. Rural-heavy. Recent Medicaid expansion (2023).

states.name: North Dakota
states.notes: Blue Cross Blue Shield North Dakota, Sanford. Rural-heavy. Low MA penetration.

states.name: Alaska
states.notes: Very low MA penetration (~10%). Limited carrier choice. Premera Blue Cross primary. Tribal health unique. Many borough/census areas instead of counties.

states.name: Vermont
states.notes: Blue Cross Blue Shield Vermont, MVP. State-based ACA exchange. Very small population.

states.name: District of Columbia
states.notes: Kaiser operates here. Aetna, AmeriHealth. Small population. State-based ACA exchange (DC Health Link). Treat as a single-area entity, skip countyVariance.
